title: How to Extract Clean Content From Any URL in 3 Lines of Python
title_hi: How to Extract Clean Content From Any URL in 3 Lines of Python
summary:
Have you ever needed to grab the actual content from a web page — without all the ads, nav bars, and JavaScript junk?

W
content:
Have you ever needed to grab the actual content from a web page — without all the ads, nav bars, and JavaScript junk?

Whether you're building an AI training pipeline, a reading app, or a data scraper, extracting clean text from arbitrary URLs is a universal pain point.

Here's the fastest way I've found.


  
  
  The Problem


Most websites are 70% boilerplate. Headers, footers, sidebars, cookie banners, popups. If you just use requests.get() and parse the HTML, you get garbage:



import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

resp = requests.get("https://example.com/article")
soup = BeautifulSoup(resp.text, "html.parser")
print(soup.get_text())
# Output: navigation menu sidebar footer ads copyright...







  
  
  The Solution: One API Call


Instead of fighting with HTML parsing, use a dedicated content extraction API:



import requests

resp = requests.post("https://web2md-api-production-d822.up.railway.app/extract", json={
    "url": "https://www.python.org/doc/",
    "format": "markdown"
})
data = resp.json()
print(data["content"][:500])






That's it. Three lines. The API handles:


Fetching the page
Stripping all boilerplate (nav, ads, scripts)
Extracting the main article content
Converting to clean Markdown



  
  
  What You Get Back


The API returns structured JSON:



{
  "success": true,
  "title": "Documentation — Python.org",
  "content": "# Markdown content here...",
  "description": "The official home of the Python Programming Language",
  "word_count": 1234,
  "response_time_ms": 850
}







  
  
  Format Options




markdown (default) — perfect for LLM training data, note-taking, publishing

json — structured paragraphs, headings, and metadata

text — plain text when you just need the words



  
  
  Pricing


It's cheap. Free tier handles 50 requests/day — enough for most side projects.


Free: 50 requests/day
Basic: 5,000/month — $4.99
Pro: 50,000/month — $19.99



  
  
  Real Use Cases




AI Training Data — Feed clean web content into your LLM fine-tuning pipeline

Research — Download articles for offline reading and analysis

Content Monitoring — Track changes on competitor pages

Reading Apps — Convert any article to a clean reading view



  
  
  Why Not Just Use BeautifulSoup?


You can, but:


Every site has different HTML structure
JavaScript-rendered content won't appear
You'll spend hours writing selectors for each site
Maintenance nightmare when sites redesign


A dedicated extraction API handles all of this with one endpoint. No selectors, no maintenance, no hassle.




This article was written with the help of AI. The API mentioned is a personal project — I built it because I needed it myself.

title: Every React Project Develops a Domain Language Over Time. The AI Invents Its Own Every Session.
title_hi: Every React Project Develops a Domain Language Over Time. The AI Invents Its Own Every Session.
summary:
Every product has a language.

Not a programming language. A domain language. The specific words that mean specific thin
content:
Every product has a language.

Not a programming language. A domain language. The specific words that mean specific things in the context of this particular product, this particular business, this particular problem space.

In an e-commerce platform a Customer is not the same as a User. An Order is not the same as a Cart. A Listing is not the same as a Product. These distinctions matter. They reflect real differences in the business domain. The right word in the right place makes code readable. The wrong word, or five different words for the same concept, makes code that requires translation every time someone reads it.

Your team knows this language. You have been building it for months or years. It lives in conversations, in pull request comments, in the names your product managers use when they describe features. It is the shared vocabulary that makes your team efficient.

Your AI does not know any of it. And every session it invents its own.


  
  
  What domain language drift actually looks like


It starts subtly and accumulates over time.

In the first few months the AI generates components and hooks that use reasonable naming. Nothing obviously wrong. The words it chooses are generic React vocabulary and they work well enough for what they describe.

But beneath the surface the domain language is fragmenting. The concept your team calls a Customer appears as User in some components, Client in others, Buyer in the ones the AI generated when it was looking at a different part of the codebase. The concept your team calls an Order appears as Purchase in one hook, Transaction in another, Checkout in a third.

None of these are wrong in isolation. User is a reasonable word. So is Client. So is Buyer. But they are not the same word and in your domain they do not mean the same thing. The AI does not know the difference because nobody told it.

Over six months of sessions, the codebase stops speaking one language. It speaks five. The AI's language for this week's feature, the AI's language from three months ago, what the senior developer wrote before the AI was in the workflow, what the new developer added last sprint, and the actual domain language that exists in business conversations but has never been written down in a form the AI could follow.

A new developer joins and tries to understand the codebase by reading it. They find Customer in some places and User in others and cannot tell if they are the same thing or different things. They have to ask. The answer is that they are the same thing. The AI just did not know.


  
  
  Why domain language matters more than naming conventions


Naming conventions are about form. PascalCase for components. camelCase for functions. handleX for event handlers. These are rules about how words are written.

Domain language is about meaning. Customer versus User. Order versus Purchase. Listing versus Product. These are decisions about which words carry which concepts.

Both matter. But they solve different problems. Naming conventions solve consistency of form. Domain language solves consistency of meaning. And consistency of meaning is what makes a codebase readable to someone who understands the business.

A codebase with perfect naming conventions but fragmented domain language is technically consistent but semantically confusing. You know how the words are capitalized. You do not know which word to use when you are describing a person who has completed a purchase.

Domain language fragmentation is harder to notice than naming convention violations because it does not trigger any automated checks. No linter catches the fact that Customer and User are being used interchangeably. No TypeScript error appears when a component receives a User object and calls it a Customer internally. The code compiles. The tests pass. The language is fragmented and nobody knows.


  
  
  Where the AI's invented language comes from


The AI is not making random choices when it names domain concepts.

It is making locally reasonable choices based on whatever context is visible in the current session. If the file it is working in uses User, it uses User. If the adjacent hook uses Customer, it might switch. If the prompt mentions a buyer, it might use Buyer. If it cannot infer anything from context, it falls back to the most generic available term.

Each individual choice is defensible. The aggregate is chaos.

This is the core of the problem. The AI is not bad at naming. It is excellent at locally consistent naming within a session. The problem is that locally consistent naming across different sessions, with different context, making different locally reasonable choices, produces a codebase that is globally inconsistent in meaning.

Domain language is by definition not something the AI can derive from context alone. Context tells it what React patterns are visible nearby. It does not tell it that in this product, in this business domain, Customer means someone who has completed at least one purchase while User means anyone with an account. That distinction exists in the business. It has never been written down in a form the AI can use before it generates something.


  
  
  Building the domain language document


The fix is not complicated. It is just work that almost no team does because it does not feel like a development task.

A domain language document is a list of the concepts in your product and the specific words your codebase uses to represent them. Not a business glossary. Not user-facing terminology. The specific technical terms that should appear in component names, hook names, type definitions, and variable names throughout the codebase.

Here is what a domain language document looks like for a simplified e-commerce context:


Domain language rules for this project:

Person concepts:
- User: anyone with an account, authenticated or not
- Customer: a User who has completed at least one purchase
- Guest: an unauthenticated visitor
- Never use: Client, Buyer, Shopper, Member

Transaction concepts:
- Cart: items selected but not yet purchased
- Order: a completed purchase transaction
- LineItem: a single product within a Cart or Order
- Never use: Basket, Purchase, Transaction, Item (alone)

Product concepts:
- Product: the base catalog entry
- Listing: a Product with pricing and availability for a specific context
- Variant: a specific configuration of a Product
- Never use: Item, Good, Merchandise, SKU (in component names)




This document does not take long to create for a project that has been running for any meaningful time. The domain language already exists. It lives in conversations and pull request comments and the names product managers use. Writing it down is mostly a matter of making explicit what is already implicit.

Once it exists it becomes part of the rules the AI receives before every session. The AI stops inventing its own vocabulary because the vocabulary has been defined. Customer appears where Customer should appear. User appears where User should appear. The distinction is maintained because the rule exists.


  
  
  What changes in the codebase over time


Domain language consistency compounds in the same way that domain language fragmentation does. But in the right direction.

When the AI uses the correct domain terms consistently, new components look like existing components not just structurally but semantically. A developer reading a new feature written last week can immediately understand what it is talking about because the words are the same words used everywhere else.

The cognitive overhead of reading the codebase drops. Not because the code became simpler. Because the language became consistent. A developer does not have to translate between User and Customer and Client when reading different parts of the system. They encounter one word for each concept and they know immediately what it means.

Onboarding accelerates. A new developer learns the domain language once from the documentation and then sees it applied consistently everywhere in the codebase, including in every component and hook the AI generates from that point forward.

Search becomes reliable. A developer looking for everything related to Orders can search for Order and find it. They do not have to also search for Purchase and Transaction and Checkout and Fulfillment and hope they have covered all the variations.

The codebase starts reading like it was written by a team that shared a language. Because the rules ensure that it was. Even when the AI was involved in building large portions of it.


  
  
  The language your product already has


Your product already has a domain language. Your team already knows it.

The gap is not that the language does not exist. The gap is that it has never been written down in a form the AI can follow before it generates something.

Every time the AI uses the wrong word for a concept in your domain, it is not making a mistake. It is filling a gap you left open. The word you wanted was not in the rules. So it used the word that seemed most reasonable given the context it had.

Write the domain language down. Give it to the AI before every session. And stop finding five words for the same concept in a codebase that should only ever have one.


  
  
  The prompt does not matter. The rules do.


Your React project has a language. It developed over time through the work your team did and the conversations you had about what things should be called.

Your AI speaks a different language every session. Not because it cannot speak yours. Because you never told it what yours was.

Write it down. Apply it consistently. And let the codebase finally speak one language instead of five.





  
  
  Want to find where your React project's domain language has fragmented?

Selling crypto to cover an unexpected expense can cost you far more than the cash you receive. Between capital gains tax, missed upside, and the psychological weight of exiting a position you believed in, the real price of selling is often hidden.
An instant crypto loan gives you access to $1,500 in cash without selling your digital assets. You borrow against your Bitcoin or Ethereum, keep your holdings intact, and avoid triggering capital gains tax . It's the same strategy wealthy investors have used for decades with stocks and real estate—access liquidity without liquidating.


In this guide, you'll learn exactly how a $1,500 instant crypto loan can give you cash with zero capital loss. We'll cover how borrowing preserves your position, how tax deferral works, and how innovative new loan products can protect you from liquidation risk.
How Borrowing Against Crypto Preserves Your Capital]
When you take out a crypto-backed loan, you deposit digital assets as collateral and receive cash in return. Your crypto stays in a secure wallet—you never sell it. At the end of the loan term, you repay the principal plus interest, and your full collateral is returned to you.
Why this means zero capital loss:
First, you keep your upside exposure. If your Bitcoin or Ethereum appreciates while your loan is outstanding, you capture every dollar of that gain. Selling would lock in your position at today's price, potentially costing you thousands in future appreciation.
Second, you avoid capital gains tax. Borrowing against crypto is not a sale under current guidance—no gain, no loss means no tax bill . This is a significant advantage if you bought at a low price and your assets have appreciated substantially. Selling could trigger tax on 18-24% of your gains; borrowing avoids it entirely.
Third, you stay invested. The "Buy, Borrow, Die" strategy used by ultra-wealthy investors works for crypto too: buy and hold long-term, borrow against your assets for liquidity, and defer taxes indefinitely.
Key Advantages of a Crypto Loan with No Capital Loss]
A $1,500 instant crypto loan offers several advantages when you want to preserve capital:
No Taxable Event. Selling crypto triggers capital gains tax. Borrowing doesn't. Under current IRS guidance, a loan secured by crypto is not a sale, so you defer taxes until you eventually sell. In the UK, new "no gain, no loss" rules will defer CGT on qualifying crypto loans starting in 2027.
Keep 100% of Your Upside. Your collateral remains yours. If your crypto's value doubles while you're repaying the loan, those gains belong entirely to you.
No Credit Check. Because your loan is fully secured by collateral, your credit history doesn't matter. Approval is based on your digital assets, not your credit score.
Competitive Interest Rates. Rates start as low as 7.25% APR on some platforms—often lower than personal loans and much lower than credit cards.
Fast Funding. Most platforms approve loans instantly once collateral is confirmed. Funds can arrive within minutes or hours.

This is the most important question. A traditional crypto loan comes with liquidation risk: if your collateral's value drops, your loan-to-value (LTV) ratio rises. Most platforms issue a margin call when LTV hits 70-80%, and liquidate collateral when it reaches 80-90%.
New products are changing this. Strike recently launched "volatility-proof" Bitcoin-backed loans that remove price-based liquidation entirely. As long as you make your scheduled payments, your Bitcoin stays yours—even if the price crashes. The trade-off is a lower initial LTV (45%) and a higher APR premium, but no forced liquidations.
How to protect yourself:
Borrow at a conservative LTV (40-50% instead of 60-70%)
Keep surplus reserves available to respond to margin calls
Consider lenders with no-rehypothecation policies—your collateral stays segregated
Platforms like Arch Lending offer competitive rates with strict no-rehypothecation policies and insured custody through federally chartered banks. Some lenders, like Coinbase, now allow borrowing against a wider range of assets including Solana.

title: How Automation Is Transforming Cyber Security Operations
title_hi: How Automation Is Transforming Cyber Security Operations
summary: Cyber attacks are becoming more frequent, complex, and difficult to manage. Security teams are expected to monitor thous
content:
Cyber attacks are becoming more frequent, complex, and difficult to manage. Security teams are expected to monitor thousands of alerts, identify real threats, and respond quickly without disrupting business operations. As organizations continue to expand their digital infrastructure, handling every security task manually is no longer practical. This is where automation is changing the way cyber security operations work.

Businesses are increasingly adopting automated security solutions to reduce response times, improve accuracy, and strengthen their overall defense strategy. This shift has also increased the demand for professionals with practical skills gained through cyber security training in India, where learners are trained to work with modern security tools, threat detection platforms, and incident response techniques.


  
  
  What Is Cyber Security Automation?


Cyber security automation is the use of software, artificial intelligence, and predefined workflows to perform security tasks with minimal human intervention. Instead of spending hours reviewing logs or responding to repetitive alerts, security teams can automate routine activities and focus on investigating high-priority threats.

Automation supports several security functions, including:


Threat detection
Log analysis
Vulnerability scanning
Incident response
Malware detection
Security reporting
User access management


By automating repetitive tasks, organizations improve efficiency while reducing the chances of human error.


  
  
  Why Automation Matters in Cyber Security Operations


Modern businesses generate massive amounts of security data every day. Firewalls, endpoints, cloud applications, and network devices continuously produce logs and alerts. Security analysts often struggle to review everything manually.

Automation helps organizations:


Detect threats faster
Respond to incidents in real time
Reduce alert fatigue
Improve security team productivity
Maintain consistent security processes
Strengthen compliance monitoring


This allows security professionals to spend more time solving complex security problems rather than performing repetitive activities.


  
  
  Key Areas Where Automation Is Making a Difference



  
  
  Faster Threat Detection


Security platforms continuously monitor networks, devices, and applications for unusual activity. Automated systems quickly compare events against threat intelligence feeds and predefined rules.

Instead of waiting for manual investigation, suspicious activities are flagged immediately, allowing security teams to respond much faster.


  
  
  Automated Incident Response


Once a threat is detected, automated workflows can begin responding without waiting for human approval in certain situations.

For example, automation can:


Isolate infected devices
Disable compromised user accounts
Block malicious IP addresses
Remove harmful files
Notify security teams instantly


This reduces the time attackers have to move within an organization's network.


  
  
  Continuous Vulnerability Management


Organizations regularly discover new software vulnerabilities. Manually checking every system is time-consuming and inefficient.

Automation continuously scans systems, identifies weaknesses, prioritizes critical risks, and generates reports that help security teams fix problems before attackers exploit them.


  
  
  Better Security Monitoring


Security Operations Centers (SOCs) receive thousands of alerts every day. Many of these alerts are false positives.

Automation filters unnecessary notifications, groups related alerts together, and highlights the most critical incidents. This helps analysts focus on genuine security threats instead of spending valuable time reviewing low-risk events.


  
  
  Improved Compliance


Many industries must comply with security standards and regulations. Automation helps organizations maintain compliance by collecting logs, monitoring configurations, generating audit reports, and identifying policy violations automatically.

This simplifies audits and reduces manual documentation work.


  
  
  Benefits of Automation for Security Teams


Automation offers several practical advantages for cyber security operations.


  
  
  Faster Response Times


Automated systems react within seconds, reducing the time between threat detection and response.


  
  
  Reduced Human Error


Manual security processes can sometimes lead to mistakes. Automation follows predefined rules consistently, helping organizations maintain reliable security operations.


  
  
  Better Use of Security Resources


Cyber security professionals can focus on advanced investigations, threat hunting, and security improvements instead of repetitive operational tasks.


  
  
  Scalable Security Operations


As organizations grow, their security workloads increase. Automation makes it easier to manage larger networks without needing a proportional increase in security staff.


  
  
  Challenges of Cyber Security Automation


Although automation provides significant advantages, it is not a complete replacement for human expertise.

Some challenges include:


Incorrect automation rules may create unnecessary actions.
Complex attacks still require experienced analysts.
Automated systems need regular updates and monitoring.
Organizations must carefully integrate automation with existing security tools.


The best results come from combining automation with skilled cyber security professionals who can make informed decisions during advanced security incidents.


  
  
  Skills Needed for an Automated Security Environment


As automation becomes a standard part of cyber security operations, professionals need a broader range of technical skills.

Important skills include:


Network security
SIEM platforms
Security automation tools
Incident response
Threat intelligence
Cloud security
Scripting basics
Vulnerability assessment
Security monitoring


Professionals who understand both security fundamentals and automation technologies are becoming increasingly valuable across industries.


  
  
  The Future of Automated Cyber Security


Automation will continue to evolve with artificial intelligence, machine learning, and predictive analytics. Future security systems will become even more effective at identifying unknown threats, reducing response times, and supporting security analysts with intelligent recommendations.

However, human expertise will remain essential. Security professionals will continue making strategic decisions, investigating sophisticated attacks, and improving organizational security strategies. Automation is becoming a powerful assistant rather than a replacement for experienced cyber security teams.

Automation is transforming cyber security operations by making threat detection, incident response, vulnerability management, and security monitoring faster and more efficient. Organizations can reduce repetitive work, improve response times, and strengthen their overall security posture through intelligent automation. As cyber threats continue to evolve, combining advanced automation with skilled professionals will remain the most effective approach to protecting modern digital environments.

SKILLOGIC Institute offers the Cyber Security Professional Plus Course, designed to prepare learners with practical skills in ethical hacking, network security, cloud security, SOC operations, incident response, SIEM, vulnerability assessment, and security automation concepts through hands-on labs and real-world projects. The program is accredited by NASSCOM FutureSkills and IIFIS, adding recognized certifications that enhance career opportunities. Learners can choose classroom training at major offline centers including Bangalore, Hyderabad, and Pune, along with online learning and career support to help them build a successful cyber security career.

Borrow a flexible $1,500 line against your digital assets. No fixed repayment schedule, pay interest only on what you use, and keep your crypto safe

You need access to cash. But you don't want to borrow a fixed amount and start paying interest on money you might not even use for weeks. That's the problem with traditional crypto loans—they force you to commit upfront .

A flexible crypto credit line changes that. Instead of a one-time loan with a fixed amount and repayment schedule, you get a revolving line of credit secured by your digital assets . Withdraw exactly what you need, when you need it. Pay interest only on the funds you actually use .
In this guide, you'll learn how to borrow a flexible $1,500 line against your digital assets safely. We'll cover how credit lines differ from fixed loans, what makes a lender secure, and how to protect your collateral.
How a Flexible Crypto Credit Line Works]
A crypto credit line replaces the fixed loan with a revolving structure . Instead of receiving a lump sum, you open a borrowing limit backed by your crypto collateral. Funds can be drawn, repaid, and reused within that limit .
Here's how it works in practice: You deposit digital assets like Bitcoin or Ethereum into a secure wallet controlled by the lender. The platform assigns you a borrowing limit based on your collateral's value, determined by the loan-to-value (LTV) ratio . For example, at a 50% LTV, $3,000 in crypto gives you a $1,500 credit line .
The key difference from a fixed loan is flexibility. With a fixed crypto loan, interest accrues on the full borrowed amount from day one, and repayment schedules are often predefined . With a credit line, interest applies only to the portion you actually withdraw . Unused credit costs nothing . Repaid amounts restore your available borrowing capacity, and there's no fixed repayment schedule .
This structure turns borrowing from a rigid commitment into a continuous liquidity layer . You can draw funds when needed, repay when conditions change, and reuse 
Key Benefits of Borrowing Against Digital Assets]
Choosing a flexible crypto credit line offers several advantages over fixed loans or selling your assets:
Pay Interest Only on What You Use. If you have access to a $1,500 line but only borrow $500, you pay interest only on that $500 . The remaining $1,000 stays available at no cost .
No Fixed Repayment Schedule. Unlike fixed loans that impose monthly payment deadlines, credit lines let you repay partially or fully whenever it suits you . No pressure to liquidate assets prematurely .

title: AI in Computing
title_hi: AI in Computing
summary:
"Maybe we were never paying for intelligence. Maybe we've always been renting computation."







Yesterday, I was tak
content:
"Maybe we were never paying for intelligence. Maybe we've always been renting computation."







Yesterday, I was taking a dump while watching SpaceX's 13th test flight.

As one does.

Somewhere between watching another rocket leave Earth and seeing yet another post about Grok getting better, I found myself thinking about something completely different.

Not AI.

Not the latest model.

Not benchmarks.

Not who cooked who on X yesterday.

But... computing.


What does computing actually mean in an AI economy?


For years, computing felt invisible. I wrote some code, deployed it somewhere, and trusted that the cloud would magically do its thing. CPUs became faster, GPUs became more powerful, data centers became larger, but I never really stopped to think about what was actually happening underneath all of it.

Then AI happened.

Suddenly every prompt had a price.

Every generated image.

Every line of code.

Every research session.

Every "thinking..." animation.

Somewhere in the world, a warehouse full of GPUs wakes up just to answer a question that started with someone typing a few words into a textbox.

That's when something clicked for me.

The interesting part isn't that AI is expensive.

The interesting part is that compute has quietly become the product.









We like to say we're paying for AI.

I'm not sure we are.

I think we're paying for access to an absurd amount of computation that's been wrapped inside an intelligent interface.

The intelligence is what we see.

The compute is what we pay for.


If every token costs compute... are tokens slowly becoming a currency?


The more I thought about it, the weirder the entire industry became.

Microsoft invests in OpenAI.

Amazon pours billions into Anthropic.

Google competes with Anthropic while also investing in it.

NVIDIA sells chips to every single one of them.

They're partners.

They're competitors.

They're customers.

They're suppliers.

At the same time.

It feels less like companies fighting each other and more like one giant ecosystem where everyone is simultaneously helping and competing with everyone else.

It's honestly funny when you stop and think about it.






Everyone is connected. Everyone is competing.





What happens when everyone depends on everyone else?


I don't think we're witnessing an AI race anymore.

I think we're witnessing a compute race.

Models will continue getting better.

Interfaces will continue changing.

Benchmarks will continue being broken every few weeks.

But underneath all of that, the real question feels much simpler.

Who owns the infrastructure capable of producing intelligence at scale?

Maybe that's why countries are suddenly racing to build data centers.

Maybe that's why energy has become part of AI conversations.

Maybe that's why GPU supply chains make headlines.

The conversation isn't really about AI anymore.

It's about everything underneath AI.




As software engineers and students, I don't think our job is simply to keep learning the newest framework or whichever model released yesterday.

AI already contains an unbelievable amount of human knowledge.

Our job is different.

To build with it.

To question it.

To innovate alongside it.

Most importantly, to make sure we don't slowly outsource the parts that make us human in the first place.

Our curiosity.

Our creativity.

Our culture.

Our judgment.

Because if intelligence becomes abundant...

Those might end up being the only things that remain scarce.









I don't really have a conclusion.

I'm still trying to make sense of all of this myself.

But I keep coming back to the same question.


If computation is becoming the world's most valuable resource, what does that make software engineers?

title: How Detailed UI Prompts Fix the 'Same App' Problem in AI Builders
title_hi: How Detailed UI Prompts Fix the 'Same App' Problem in AI Builders
summary: Open five apps built with an AI app builder this month and look closely. There's a decent chance they feel related to ea
content:
Open five apps built with an AI app builder this month and look closely. There's a decent chance they feel related to each other, similar hero sections, the same shade of blue on the primary button, a three-column feature grid with icons from the same set. Different product, same bones.

This isn't really about the model being limited. It's about what a prompt does and doesn't give it to work with.


  
  
  Why do so many AI-built interfaces look the same?


Large language models generate interfaces by predicting the most statistically likely output for the instruction they're given. When that instruction is vague "make it clean and modern," "build me a dashboard" the model has nothing to anchor its choices to, so it reaches for whatever pattern shows up most often in its training data. One breakdown of this behavior puts it plainly: vague prompts don't fail because the AI lacks taste, they fail because they never asked for one. A related piece on prompting for design work makes a similar point when a brief is vague, the model "averages," producing output that's technically correct and visually forgettable.

The fix isn't a smarter model. It's a more detailed prompt.


  
  
  What actually makes a UI prompt detailed enough?



  
  
  1. Name who the screen is for, not just what it does


"Build a fitness app" tells the model nothing about who's opening it or why. Naming a specific persona, device, and use case up front gives the model something to design around instead of guessing.


  
  
  2. Describe structure the way you'd describe a floor plan


Instead of "a dashboard," describe what's actually on it how many metric cards, what kind of chart, how many rows in the activity table. A widely referenced comparison of vague-versus-detailed prompting shows a genuinely detailed prompt for a trip-planner screen spelling out layout, color-coding, and exact click interactions before any code gets written.


  
  
  3. Replace "clean and modern" with a comparison point


Every app claims to be clean and modern, which is exactly why the phrase produces nothing distinctive. Naming a reference point instead "styled like Stripe," or a specific palette and tone, works better. Guidance on prompting tools like v0 frames this as writing a creative brief rather than a request.


  
  
  4. Paste in the actual content, not a placeholder for it


Generic filler text produces generic layouts, because the model designs around whatever it's given. A structured five-part prompt format for AI design and code generators recommends supplying real placeholder copy and sample data up front so layout decisions respond to actual content.


  
  
  5. Build the screen in phases instead of one giant ask


Overloading a single prompt with navigation, forms, and visual polish all at once tends to produce something that works but is structurally messy. A guide to prompting AI app builders recommends a component-first approach instead skeleton, then function, then polish. Prompts that try to do too much at once are a recurring source of interfaces that look right but are hard to build on later, according to a piece on avoiding UI refactors in AI-coded projects.


  
  
  Is this becoming a workflow choice instead of a prompting habit?


Writing five detailed prompts every time works, but it depends on someone remembering to do it before hitting generate. That's part of why some AI builders are starting to move this planning step into the product itself. 8080.ai's approach is one example: before any interface code is generated, a planning pass maps screens per persona, and the home page is designed and approved first so the rest of the app generates against a style that's already been signed off, instead of each screen guessing independently. Other multi-agent builders are experimenting with similar review gates and design-critique passes for the same reason specificity holds up better when it's structural rather than something a person has to remember every time.

That's the underlying shift. The interfaces that look intentional aren't coming from a magic phrase. They're coming from prompts written like design briefs and, increasingly, from tools built to hold that discipline even when the person typing forgets to.

title: Laravel + AI: Building Smarter Applications with Laravel 13's Native AI SDK
title_hi: Laravel + AI: Building Smarter Applications with Laravel 13's Native AI SDK
summary: Laravel has always made hard things simple - routing, queues, authentication, and now, AI. With Laravel 13, artificial i
content:
Laravel has always made hard things simple - routing, queues, authentication, and now, AI. With Laravel 13, artificial intelligence moves from "something you bolt on with a third-party package" to a first-class citizen of the framework itself. If you're a business evaluating how to add AI features to your product without hiring a separate ML team, this update changes the calculus.


  
  
  What's Actually New: The Laravel AI SDK


Laravel 13 introduces a first-party AI SDK, giving developers one unified API for:



Text generation - chat completions, content generation, summarization

Tool-calling agents - AI that can call functions/APIs to take real actions, not just respond with text

Embeddings - turning text into vectors for search and recommendation features

Audio and image generation - multimodal capabilities without separate SDKs

Vector-store integrations - built-in support for storing and querying embeddings


Why this matters: Previously, adding AI to a Laravel app meant stitching together multiple third-party packages (each with its own auth pattern, error handling, and versioning quirks). Now it's one consistent, framework-native interface.


  
  
  Why a Native SDK Beats a Bolt-On Package





  
  
  Practical Use Cases for Businesses



  
  
  1. AI-Powered Customer Support


Tool-calling agents can look up order status, check inventory, or escalate tickets - connected directly to your existing Laravel models and database, no separate service layer needed.


  
  
  2. Smarter Search


Embeddings + vector-store integration let you build semantic search (search by meaning, not just keyword match) directly inside your Laravel app - useful for e-commerce catalogs, knowledge bases, or content-heavy platforms.


  
  
  3. Internal Automation Agents


Since agents can call tools/functions, you can build internal agents that handle repetitive workflows - invoice processing, lead qualification, report generation - using your app's existing business logic.


  
  
  4. Content and Media Features


Native text, audio, and image generation support means features like auto-generated product descriptions, alt text, or audio summaries no longer require a separate microservice.


  
  
  What This Means If You're Building or Migrating



New projects: Starting fresh on Laravel 13 means AI features can be planned into the architecture from day one, not retrofitted later
Existing Laravel apps: If you're on Laravel 11 or 12, the AI SDK is a strong reason to plan an upgrade path - [Laravel 12 continues receiving bug fixes until August 2026 and security fixes until February 2027], so there's no immediate pressure, but earlier adoption means earlier feature parity with competitors building AI-native products
PHP requirement: Laravel 13 requires PHP 8.3 minimum — worth checking your hosting/server environment supports this before planning a migration



  
  
  Getting Started the Right Way


Adding AI to a production application isn't just "call the API and ship it." Teams still need to think through:


Data privacy and what gets sent to AI providers
Fallback behavior when AI calls fail or return low-confidence results
Cost management for API-based AI features at scale
Testing strategies for non-deterministic AI outputs



  
  
  Conclusion


Laravel 13's AI SDK closes the gap between "framework" and "AI-ready platform." For businesses already on Laravel, it's a strong argument to modernize. For businesses evaluating a new build, it's a reason to consider Laravel over frameworks where AI integration still means assembling your own toolkit.

title: Your website mostly keeps working. Your app decays on someone else's calendar.
title_hi: Your website mostly keeps working. Your app decays on someone else's calendar.
summary: Disclosure: I work with ZoopCoder, an Indian development agency that sells maintenance plans. So take the sales-shaped p
content:
Disclosure: I work with ZoopCoder, an Indian development agency that sells maintenance plans. So take the sales-shaped parts with appropriate salt — but every number below is published, checkable, and I've included the cases where you should buy nothing at all.

There's an asymmetry in maintenance that almost no quote explains, and it catches technical founders as often as non-technical ones:

A website left alone mostly keeps working. An app left alone stops being installable.

Same neglect, completely different outcome. Nothing in your code changed in either case. The difference is whose calendar the decay runs on — and once you see it that way, "do I need a maintenance plan?" stops being one question and becomes two.


  
  
  The website case: decay is mostly optional


A static brochure site — no logins, no payments, no database, no plugins — has almost nothing to rot. Renew the domain, keep hosting paid, and it will serve the same HTML in 2028 that it serves today. Let's Encrypt renews the certificate itself.

So the honest floor for keeping a small site alive in India is not a monthly retainer. It's this:




Item
Typical cost per year (India, 2026)
Actually required?




Domain renewal (.com / .in)
₹800 – ₹1,500
Yes, always


Shared hosting
₹2,000 – ₹6,000
Enough for a brochure or small WordPress site


VPS / cloud hosting
₹6,000 – ₹30,000
Only for custom apps, real traffic, or live orders


SSL certificate
₹0
Free via Let's Encrypt. Never pay for a basic cert


Business email
₹0 – ₹2,000 / user
Optional


Floor — small business site
₹3,000 – ₹8,000 / year
Domain + shared hosting. That's the whole bill


Floor — custom app on a VPS
₹7,000 – ₹32,000 / year
Domain + a real server




₹3,000–₹8,000 per year is the floor. Compare that to a maintenance plan at ₹3,000–₹15,000+ per month and you can see why the distinction matters: those two numbers differ by more than an order of magnitude, and plenty of quotes blur them together deliberately.

The moment the site has moving parts, the maths flips hard. A WordPress or WooCommerce install is a live security exposure — an unpatched one is the single most common way Indian small-business sites get defaced, and cleaning up one hack costs more than a year of a basic plan. But that's a conditional cost driven by what you built, not an inevitability.


  
  
  The app case: decay runs on Google's and Apple's calendar


Apps carry two costs websites don't, and one of them has teeth:




Cost
Amount
What happens if you stop paying




Google Play Console
$25 one-time (~₹2,200)
Nothing. There is no renewal — pay once, ever


Apple Developer Program
$99 per year (~₹8,700)
Your app is removed from the App Store





(Rupee figures are approximate and move with the exchange rate — the fees themselves are set in USD. Verify current fees with Google and Apple directly.)

But the fees aren't the interesting part. This is:

Google and Apple raise their minimum SDK, privacy and permission requirements on their own schedule, not yours.

An app left untouched for two years will eventually fail a store requirement and stop being installable — even though nothing about it broke, no dependency rotted, and you changed nothing. You didn't incur technical debt. The platform moved the floor underneath a binary that was, by every local measure, fine.

That's a genuinely different failure mode from "the code got old", and it's why "it's finished, we can stop paying" is a reasonable thing to say about a brochure site and a dangerous thing to say about an app. The website's clock is yours. The app's clock belongs to two companies in California who have never heard of you.


  
  
  What plans actually cost, and where they sit in the Indian market


These are our published plans, not an illustrative example:




Plan
Per month
Year one (11 mo)
Right for




Post-launch support
₹0 (first 30 days)
—
Included with every project


Basic
₹3,000
₹33,000
Brochure sites, small WordPress sites


Professional
₹8,000
₹88,000
E-commerce, lead-gen, anything with live orders


Enterprise
₹15,000+
₹1,65,000+
SaaS, CRM, ERP, business-critical platforms




Year one multiplies by 11, not 12, because the first 30 days post-launch are included — you're not paying a retainer during the month you're most likely to need one.

Add the floor to the plan and you get the real number: a small WordPress site on Basic is about ₹39,000 in year one (₹33,000 + ~₹6,000 renewals) and ~₹42,000/year after. A busy store on Professional is about ₹95,000 in year one. A SaaS on Enterprise starts near ₹1,90,000/year. Those are the numbers to budget — not the plan price alone.

For market context: published Indian agency rates in 2026 run ~₹1,500/month for a basic site to ₹50,000+/month for enterprise platforms, with e-commerce typically ₹10,000–₹20,000/month. Our Professional tier at ₹8,000 sits below that band. I'd rather say that plainly than pretend we're the cheapest at every tier — we aren't, and the Enterprise "+" is real, because scope sets the number and an SLA costs what it costs.


  
  
  The percentage trap, briefly


Many Indian agencies quote an AMC as 15–20% of build cost per year. It sounds principled. It's the wrong shape.

Maintenance effort is driven by how often the thing changes, how much custom code it carries, and how much traffic and risk it handles. Your build price is none of those — it's a number from the past. So it fails in both directions:




Scenario
Build cost
AMC at 18%
What the work is worth
Result




Expensive site, barely changes
₹5,00,000
₹90,000/yr
Renewals + a few hours
You overpay, forever


Cheap build, became a busy store
₹40,000
₹7,200/yr
Live orders, monitoring, fast response
You're underserved, and won't be told




Row two is the dangerous one. ₹7,200/year is ₹600/month. Nobody monitors a live store for ₹600 a month. What you bought is the word maintenance, and you'll discover what it didn't include on the day something breaks.


  
  
  When to buy nothing


We sell these plans, so hold me to this: a maintenance plan is a service you choose to buy, not a bill you automatically owe. Skip it if all of these hold:


The site is static — no logins, no payments, no database, no plugins.
It changes fewer than three or four times a year, and only text and images.
No revenue depends on it being up this hour.
Someone can email a developer when it breaks.


In that case: renew domain and hosting for ₹3,000–₹8,000/year and buy ad-hoc hours when you need them. Four small changes a year bought as hours will almost always beat ₹36,000 of retainer.

Buy the plan if it runs a plugin ecosystem, takes payments, stores customer data, carries leads or orders, or is something people log into to do their job. The dividing line isn't the size of your business or what you paid to build the thing — it's whether it has moving parts, and whether money flows through it. And if it's an app, remember the clock isn't yours.


  
  
  The one clause that matters more than the price


Make sure you own the code, the domain and the hosting account. If you can leave, a maintenance plan is a service you're choosing. If you can't, it isn't a plan — it's rent on something you already bought. Check which one you're being offered before you compare prices, because that single fact determines whether the price means anything at all.

Full breakdown with the year-one arithmetic, the renewal floor and the market comparison: How much does website maintenance cost in India? · Plans are published at zoopcoder.com/pricing.php.

title: Use a Free Cloud Agent to Build an Expand–Contract Migration That Survives Mixed Versions
title_hi: Use a Free Cloud Agent to Build an Expand–Contract Migration That Survives Mixed Versions
summary: Rename users.name to display_name while an old client still writes name. The failure is an old request returning 500—or
content:
Rename users.name to display_name while an old client still writes name. The failure is an old request returning 500—or a rollback silently losing new values.

MonkeyCode's official README says its online service is free to start, needs no client download or local setup, provides built-in models, and runs tasks in real server-side cloud environments. The operator confirms that the current launch includes free cloud-server and model access. Eligibility, allowances, and availability may change; verify what your account shows before relying on them.

Sources: MonkeyCode repository and MonkeyCode Online.


  
  
  Require four phases





1 expand: add display_name, keep name
2 compatibility: dual-read and dual-write
3 backfill: copy with resumable batches
4 contract: remove name only after old writers reach zero









ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN display_name TEXT;
UPDATE users SET display_name=name WHERE display_name IS NULL;
-- Contract removal is a later deployment, never this first migration.






The acceptance matrix must run old-app/new-database, new-app/expanded-database, rollback-app/expanded-database, and a half-finished backfill. Inject an old-client write after backfill and verify it reaches both representations. Record row counts before and after rollback.



stop if: old_writer_count > 0
rollback: deploy dual-read version; retain both columns
cleanup: remove disposable DB and cloud workspace






This small database cannot model production locks, replicas, or traffic. It verifies that the generated patch understands mixed-version deployment rather than merely passing a final-schema test. Which phase needs an independent rollback threshold in your system?

title: Resolving Edit-vs-Delete Conflicts in Cloud Sessions

summary: Testing edit-vs-delete conflicts in cloud sessions
content: When testing offline edit-vs-delete conflicts in a free cloud session, a critical failure may occur. Device A edits a title offline, while Device B deletes the same record. If A reconnects first, the tombstoned record may silently become active again. To mitigate this, model the conflict without hardware and resolve it using a versioning system. The MonkeyCode online service provides a free cloud-server and model access, making it an ideal platform for testing. Sources: MonkeyCode repository and MonkeyCode Online.

title: Free Cloud Coding Agent: A Contradictory Spec
title_hi: निःशुल्क क्लाउड कोडिंग एजेंट: एक विरोधाभासी विशिष्टता
summary: Free cloud coding agent with contradictory spec
content:
Imagine giving a free cloud coding agent a contradictory spec: 'Delete immediately and permanently, but allow Undo for 24 hours.' This requirement puts the agent in a difficult position, as it must balance the need for immediate deletion with the need for reversibility. The MonkeyCode online service, which offers free cloud-server and model access, must navigate this contradiction. The operator confirms that the current launch includes free access, but eligibility and allowances may change. 
   The conflict between immediate permanent deletion and 24-hour undo is unresolved, with the consequence of destructive data loss. To resolve this, the agent must stop and ask for clarification. The allowed work is limited to tests and reversible design sketches. 
   Before reviewing code, the interaction must be scored based on signal, pass condition, detection, consequence, question, restraint, and evidence. A weak answer, such as 'just use best practice,' is insufficient and requires a product decision. A safe next artifact is a reversible state model, not a claimed final implementation.
content_hi:
एक निःशुल्क क्लाउड कोडिंग एजेंट को एक विरोधाभासी विशिष्टता देने की कल्पना करें: 'तुरंत और स्थायी रूप से हटाएं, लेकिन 24 घंटे के लिए Undo की अनुमति दें।' यह आवश्यकता एजेंट को एक कठिन स्थिति में डालती है, क्योंकि यह तुरंत हटाने की आवश्यकता के साथ उलटफेर की आवश्यकता को संतुलित करना होगा। 
   मंकीकोड ऑनलाइन सेवा, जो निःशुल्क क्लाउड-सर्वर और मॉडल एक्सेस प्रदान करती है, इस विरोधाभास को नेविगेट करना होगा। ऑपरेटर की पुष्टि है कि वर्तमान लॉन्च में निःशुल्क एक्सेस शामिल है, लेकिन पात्रता और अनुमति बदल सकती है। 
   तुरंत स्थायी हटाने और 24-घंटे के Undo के बीच संघर्ष अनसुलझा है, जिसका परिणाम विनाशकारी डेटा हानि है। इसे हल करने के लिए, एजेंट को रोकना और स्पष्टीकरण मांगना होगा। अनुमत कार्य परीक्षण और उलटी डिज़ाइन स्केच तक सीमित है।

title: Fixing Duplicate Emails with Cloud Agents
title_hi: क्लाउड एजेंट के साथ डुप्लिकेट ईमेल की समस्या का समाधान
summary: Fix duplicate emails with cloud agents
content: Imagine a scenario where a worker sends an email, crashes, and then receives the same email again, resulting in duplicate emails for the user. To tackle this issue, we can utilize a cloud agent that ensures idempotency. MonkeyCode's online service offers a free cloud-server and model access, allowing us to build a simulator and test various crash points. The simulator persists an idempotency key outside process memory and replays every point twice, emitting JSONL to track deliveries, sends, and acknowledgments. By requiring a provider idempotency key or an outbox/dispatcher contract, we can ensure atomicity between the external email provider and local state.
content_hi: एक ऐसी स्थिति की कल्पना करें जहां एक वर्कर ईमेल भेजता है, क्रैश हो जाता है, और फिर से उसी ईमेल को प्राप्त करता है, जिससे उपयोगकर्ता को डुप्लिकेट ईमेल प्राप्त होते हैं। इस समस्या का समाधान करने के लिए, हम एक क्लाउड एजेंट का उपयोग कर सकते हैं जो इडेम्पोटेंसी सुनिश्चित करता है। मोन्कोड की ऑनलाइन सेवा मुफ्त क्लाउड-सERVER और मॉडल एक्सेस प्रदान करती है, जिससे हम एक सिम्युलेटर बना सकते हैं और विभिन्न क्रैश पॉइंट्स का परीक्षण कर सकते हैं।

title: 'Wrong (again)' — Infinity Ward Fires Back at Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 Gunny AI Chatbot Claim
title_hi: इन्फिनिटी वार्ड ने कॉल ऑफ ड्यूटी: मॉडर्न वॉरफेयर 4 गनी एआई चैटबॉट दावे को गलत ठहराया
summary: Infinity Ward denies AI chatbot claim
content:
Infinity Ward has responded to claims that the new Gunsmith for Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 features an AI chatbot. The game developer denied the rumors, stating that the Gunsmith is an advanced system, but not an AI chatbot. This comes after fans speculated about the game's features, sparking a heated debate online. 
   The Gunsmith is a key feature in Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4, allowing players to customize their guns. However, the rumors of an AI chatbot have been put to rest by Infinity Ward. 
   The game is set to release soon, and fans are eagerly awaiting its launch. With the Gunsmith feature, players can expect a more immersive gaming experience.
content_hi:
इन्फिनिटी वार्ड ने कॉल ऑफ ड्यूटी: मॉडर्न वॉरफेयर 4 के नए गनस्मिथ में एआई चैटबॉट की सुविधा होने के दावों का खंडन किया है। गेम डेवलपर ने कहा कि गनस्मिथ एक उन्नत प्रणाली है, लेकिन एआई चैटबॉट नहीं है। 
   गनस्मिथ कॉल ऑफ ड्यूटी: मॉडर्न वॉरफेयर 4 की एक प्रमुख विशेषता है, जो खिलाड़ियों को अपनी बंदूकों को अनुकूलित करने की अनुमति देती है। हालांकि, एआई चैटबॉट के बारे में अफवाहें इन्फिनिटी वार्ड द्वारा गलत साबित हुई हैं। 
   गेम जल्द ही रिलीज़ होने वाला है, और प्रशंसक इसकी रिलीज़ का बेसब्री से इंतजार कर रहे हैं। गनस्मिथ फीचर के साथ, खिलाड़ियों को एक अधिक इमर्सिव गेमिंग अनुभव की उम्मीद है।

title: GTA 6 Predicted to Shatter Sales Records with $5.2 Billion in Launch Week
title_hi: जीटीए 6: लॉन्च के पहले हफ्ते में 5.2 अरब डॉलर की बिक्री की भविष्यवाणी
summary: GTA 6 sales predicted to reach $5.2 billion by launch week
content: Grand Theft Auto 6 is poised to make history with its highly anticipated release. According to analysts, the game is expected to generate a staggering $5.2 billion in sales by the end of its launch week. This prediction comes on the heels of an unprecedented preorder campaign, with the game securing an estimated $260 million in preorders. The game's developer, Rockstar Games, has been tight-lipped about the game's details, but the hype surrounding its release is undeniable. With its engaging storyline, improved graphics, and expansive gameplay, GTA 6 is set to captivate gamers worldwide.
content_hi: ग्रैंड थेफ्ट ऑटो 6 अपनी अत्यधिक प्रत्याशित रिलीज के साथ इतिहास रचने के लिए तैयार है। विश्लेषकों के अनुसार, खेल लॉन्च के पहले सप्ताह में 5.2 अरब डॉलर की बिक्री करने की उम्मीद है। यह भविष्यवाणी खेल के अनोखे प्री-ऑर्डर अभियान के बाद आई है, जिसमें अनुमानित 260 मिलियन डॉलर की प्री-ऑर्डर बिक्री हुई।

title: GTA Online Imitates Real Life with Reduced Payouts
title_hi: जीटीए ऑनलाइन में कम हुई पayouts, बढ़ी चुनौती
summary: GTA Online reduces payouts, increasing gameplay depth
content: GTA Online has taken a bold step by reducing payouts for various activities, mirroring the economic struggles of real life. This change encourages players to explore different activities, as relying on a single source of income may not be sustainable. With increased costs for in-game items and properties, players must adapt to survive. The game now demands a more diverse approach, making it essential to participate in various missions and events to maintain a stable financial standing.
content_hi: जीटीए ऑनलाइन ने एक बड़ा कदम उठाया है, जिसमें विभिन्न गतिविधियों के लिए पayouts को कम कर दिया गया है, जो वास्तविक जीवन की आर्थिक कठिनाइयों की नकल करता है। यह परिवर्तन खिलाड़ियों को विभिन्न गतिविधियों को आजमाने के लिए प्रोत्साहित करता है, क्योंकि एक ही स्रोत पर निर्भर रहना टिकाऊ नहीं हो सकता है।

title: How AI Agents Revolutionized Code Generation
title_hi: एआई एजेंट्स द्वारा कोड जनरेशन की क्रांति
summary: AI-powered code generator for web development
content: Our AI agents, Klára, Jan, Martin, and Tomáš, collaborated to create a Universal Code Snippet & Embeddable Widget Generator. This tool streamlines web component development by allowing users to visually design and generate clean, production-ready HTML/CSS/JS snippets. The generator eliminates the need for manual coding, ensuring responsiveness, cross-browser compatibility, and optimization.
content_hi: हमारे एआई एजेंट्स, क्लारा, जान, मार्टिन, और टॉमस, ने मिलकर यूनिवर्सल कोड स्निपेट और एम्बेडेबल विजेट जनरेटर बनाया। यह टूल वेब घटक विकास को सरल बनाता है, जिससे उपयोगकर्ता दृश्य रूप से डिज़ाइन कर सकते हैं और स्वच्छ, उत्पादन-तैयार HTML/CSS/JS स्निपेट जनरेट कर सकते हैं।

title: Why I Switched from Ponytail to Guardsman for AI Coding
title_hi: पोनीटेल से गार्डसमैन में क्यों स्विच किया
summary: Switch from Ponytail to Guardsman for accountable AI coding
content: I initially switched to Ponytail for AI coding, which reduced code by 54%. However, I soon discovered that while Ponytail excelled at minimizing code, it lacked accountability. This led to debugging issues later on. Guardsman, on the other hand, taught my AI agent to write accountable code, reducing the need for tedious debugging. If you're tired of AI-generated bloat or debugging disasters, consider making the switch. Ponytail's approach to minimalism sometimes resulted in oversimplification, leaving out crucial error handling and security measures. Guardsman fills this gap, ensuring that the code generated is not only efficient but also reliable and secure.
content_hi: मैंने पहले पोनीटेल को अपने एआई कोडिंग के लिए चुना था, जिसने कोड को 54% कम कर दिया। हालांकि, जल्द ही मुझे एहसास हुआ कि पोनीटेल ने जहां कोड को कम करने में उत्कृष्टता हासिल की, वहीं यह जिम्मेदारी से वंचित था। इसके परिणामस्वरूप बाद में डिबगिंग समस्याएं उत्पन्न हुईं। दूसरी ओर, गार्डसमैन ने मेरे एआई एजेंट को जिम्मेदार कोड लिखना सिखाया, जिससे डिबगिंग की आवश्यकता कम हो गई। यदि आप एआई द्वारा उत्पन्न ब्लोट या डिबगिंग आपदाओं से थक गए हैं, तो स्विच करने पर विचार करें। पोनीटेल का न्यूनतमवाद के प्रति दृष्टिकोण कभी-कभी अधिक सरलीकरण की ओर ले जाता था, जिससे महत्वपूर्ण त्रुटि हैंडलिंग और सुरक्षा उपायों की अनदेखी होती थी। गार्डसमैन इस अंतर को पूरा करता है, सुनिश्चित करता है कि उत्पन्न कोड न केवल कुशल हो, बल्कि विश्वसनीय और सुरक्षित भी हो।

title: Unveiling AI Glasses: The Future of Tech
title_hi: एआई चश्मा: तकनीक का भविष्य
summary: Explore AI glasses tech
content: AI glasses are revolutionizing the way we interact with digital information. By merging augmented reality (AR) and artificial intelligence (AI), these devices enable users to access real-time data and enhance their visual experiences. The key components of AI glasses include lightweight hardware, integration with existing AR frameworks, and on-device data processing to ensure low latency. As AI glasses continue to evolve, they raise important questions about authenticity and data privacy.
content_hi: एआई चश्मे डिजिटल जानकारी के साथ बातचीत करने के तरीके को बदल रहे हैं। ऑगमेंटेड रियलिटी (एआर) और आर्टिफिशियल इंटेलिजेंस (एआई) को मिलाकर, वे उपयोगकर्ताओं को वास्तविक समय के डेटा तक पहुंच प्रदान करते हैं और उनके दृश्य अनुभवों को बढ़ाते हैं। एआई चश्मों के मुख्य घटकों में हल्के हार्डवेयर, मौजूदा एआर फ्रेमवर्क के साथ एकीकरण और कम विलंबता सुनिश्चित करने के लिए डिवाइस पर डेटा प्रोसेसिंग शामिल है।

title: Boosting On-Device LLM Performance
title_hi: ऑन-डिवाइस एलएलएम प्रदर्शन में सुधार
summary: On-device LLM scheduling for Android
content: This tutorial guides you through building a three-layer Android inference scheduler for on-device LLMs, ensuring seamless foreground chat, inline suggestions, and background summarization without UI stalls. By leveraging priority queues, preemption, and the Android Inference Engine, you'll learn to multiplex LLM calls efficiently. The goal is to prevent in-flight job cancellations and UI freezes, even when handling concurrent requests.
content_hi: इस ट्यूटोरियल में, हम तीन-परत वाले एंड्रॉइड अनुमान अनुसूचक का निर्माण करेंगे जो ऑन-डिवाइस एलएलएम कॉल्स को फोरग्राउंड चैट, इनलाइन सुझाव और बैकग्राउंड सारांश के लिए समानांतर करता है। हम प्राथमिकता कतारों, पूर्व-स्थानांतरण और एंड्रॉइड अनुमान इंजन का उपयोग करके यूआई को रोके बिना इन अनुरोधों को संभालने का तरीका सीखेंगे।

title: Auth for Indie Hackers 2026: Clerk vs Supabase Auth vs NextAuth v5
title_hi: स्वतंत्र हैकर्स के लिए प्रमाणीकरण 2026: क्लर्क बनाम सुपाबेस प्रमाणीकरण बनाम नेक्स्टऑथ v5
summary: Auth solutions for indie hackers: Clerk, Supabase Auth, NextAuth v5
content: Stop building your own auth. In 2026, pick one of these three: Clerk, Supabase Auth, or NextAuth v5. Why waste time on problems that are 100% solved? Clerk offers a beautiful UI with 10,000 free MAU, while Supabase Auth provides DB-native integration with 50,000 free MAU. NextAuth v5 gives you full control, free forever. Don't waste your time on hashing passwords, email verification, and session management. Choose one and ship your product faster.
content_hi: अपना खुद का प्रमाणीकरण बनाना बंद करें। 2026 में, इन तीन में से एक का चयन करें: क्लर्क, सुपाबेस प्रमाणीकरण, या नेक्स्टऑथ v5। 100% हल की गई समस्याओं पर समय बर्बाद क्यों करें? क्लर्क एक सुंदर UI प्रदान करता है जिसमें 10,000 मुफ्त MAU हैं, जबकि सुपाबेस प्रमाणीकरण DB-मूल इंटीग्रेशन प्रदान करता है जिसमें 50,000 मुफ्त MAU हैं। नेक्स्टऑथ v5 आपको पूर्ण नियंत्रण देता है, जो हमेशा के लिए मुफ्त है।

title: Let the Loop Iterate: Restarting Only Pays If You Know What to Throw Away
title_hi: लूप को दोहराएं: केवल तब पुनः प्रारंभ करें जब आप जानते हों कि क्या त्यागना है
summary: Restarting coding agent attempts: does it pay off?
content: A complete study reveals the effectiveness of restarting a coding agent's attempt when it's going badly. The experiment involves 3 seeds, 3 policies, and 2 model tiers on a 29-feature slice. The results show that restarting can be beneficial, but only if the agent knows what to discard. The study builds upon a previous experiment, using a new harness that allows agents to see their test results and restart voluntarily.
content_hi: एक पूर्ण अध्ययन से पता चलता है कि कोडिंग एजेंट के प्रयास को पुनः आरंभ करने से क्या लाभ हो सकता है जब यह खराब हो रहा हो। यह प्रयोग 3 बीज, 3 नीतियों और 2 मॉडल स्तरों पर 29-विशेषता स्लाइस पर किया गया है। परिणाम बताते हैं कि पुनः आरंभ करना लाभदायक हो सकता है, लेकिन केवल तभी जब एजेंट जानता है कि क्या त्यागना है।

title: Automation Gone Wrong: A Cautionary Tale
title_hi: स्वचालन गाथा : एक सावधानी कहानी
summary: Automation fail: same article published five times
content: I recently automated my content pipeline using Claude Code, which was supposed to publish a new article every Friday. However, due to a flaw in the setup, the same article was published five times. The issue arose because the agent had no way of knowing what had been published, resulting in it picking the same article from the backlog every week. This experience highlights the importance of thorough testing and consideration of potential pitfalls when automating tasks.
content_hi: मैंने हाल ही में क्लाउड कोड का उपयोग करके अपनी सामग्री पाइपलाइन को स्वचालित किया, जो हर शुक्रवार को एक नया लेख प्रकाशित करने वाला था। हालांकि, सेटअप में एक दोष के कारण,同一个 लेख पांच बार प्रकाशित हुआ। समस्या उत्पन्न हुई क्योंकि एजेंट को यह पता नहीं था कि क्या प्रकाशित किया गया था, जिसके परिणामस्वरूप यह हर सप्ताह बैकलॉग से समान लेख चुनता था।

title: 5 Lightweight Tailscale Alternatives for Your Hardware
title_hi: अपने हार्डवेयर के लिए ५ हल्के टेल्स्केल विकल्प
summary: Tailscale alternatives for homemade hardware
content: Tailscale's WireGuard mesh is elegant, but its closed-source code and server dependency are concerns. The coordination server is a hidden dependency, handling device auth, key rotation, and peer discovery. If it goes down, your mesh doesn't degrade gracefully. For production services, this is a significant outage risk. Alternatives like Headscale, Netbird, and Nebula offer more control and flexibility.
content_hi: टेल्स्केल का वायरगार्ड मेश सुंदर है, लेकिन इसका बंद-स्रोत कोड और सर्वर निर्भरता चिंता का विषय है। समन्वय सर्वर एक छिपी हुई निर्भरता है, जो डिवाइस प्रमाणीकरण, कुंजी रोटेशन और पीयर खोज को संभालती है। यदि यह नीचे जाता है, तो आपका मेश सुंदरता से गिरता नहीं है। उत्पादन सेवाओं के लिए, यह एक महत्वपूर्ण आउटेज जोखिम है।

title: Revolutionizing Education: AI Learning for K-12
title_hi: क्रांतिकारी शिक्षा: कक्षा 12 तक के लिए एआई सीखने
summary: AI in K-12 education
content:
The integration of AI in K-12 education is transforming the learning landscape. Emerging technologies are enabling personalized learning experiences, enhancing student engagement, and improving academic outcomes. A well-designed AI learning framework can help educators create immersive and interactive lessons, making complex concepts more accessible and fun for students. 
   AI-powered adaptive learning systems can adjust the difficulty level of course materials based on individual students' needs and abilities. This approach ensures that each student receives an optimal learning experience, reducing the risk of knowledge gaps and increasing overall academic achievement. 
   As AI technology continues to evolve, it's essential to develop a comprehensive design framework that prioritizes student-centered learning, inclusivity, and teacher support.
content_hi:
कक्षा 12 तक की शिक्षा में एआई का एकीकरण सीखने के परिदृश्य को बदल रहा है। उभरती प्रौद्योगिकियां व्यक्तिगत सीखने के अनुभवों को सक्षम कर रही हैं, छात्रों की भागीदारी को बढ़ा रही हैं और अकादमिक परिणामों में सुधार कर रही हैं। एक अच्छी तरह से डिज़ाइन किए गए एआई सीखने के फ्रेमवर्क से शिक्षकों को आकर्षक और इंटरएक्टिव पाठ बनाने में मदद मिल सकती है, जिससे जटिल अवधारणाएं छात्रों के लिए अधिक सुलभ और मज़ेदार हो जाती हैं।
   एआई-संचालित अनुकूली सीखने प्रणाली पाठ्यक्रम सामग्री के कठिनाई स्तर को व्यक्तिगत छात्रों की जरूरतों और क्षमताओं के आधार पर समायोजित कर सकती हैं। यह दृष्टिकोण सुनिश्चित करता है कि प्रत्येक छात्र को एक अद्वितीय सीखने का अनुभव प्राप्त हो, ज्ञान अंतराल के जोखिम को कम करता है और समग्र अकादमिक उपलब्धि में वृद्धि करता है।
   एआई प्रौद्योगिकी के विकसित होने के साथ, यह आवश्यक है कि हम एक व्यापक डिज़ाइन फ्रेमवर्क विकसित करें जो छात्र-केंद्रित सीखने, समावेशिता और शिक्षक समर्थन पर प्राथमिकता देता है।

title: Kimi K3 Explained: Unpacking the Largest Open-Source Model
title_hi: किमी K3: विशाल ओपन-सोर्स मॉडल की व्याख्या
summary: Kimi K3: Largest open-source model explained
content: Moonshot AI has released Kimi K3, a 2.8 trillion parameter open-weight model with a 1 million token context window. This model boasts native multimodality, processing images and video in the same model. Although the weights are not yet available, the benchmarks show promising results, beating several other models. The era of dirt-cheap Chinese models may be ending, and 'open' now means something you can inspect but probably never run. The Kimi K3's architecture includes MoE and Kimi Delta Attention, making it a significant development in the AI field.
content_hi: मूनशॉट एआई ने किमी K3 जारी किया है, जिसमें 2.8 ट्रिलियन पैरामीटर ओपन-वेट मॉडल और 1 मिलियन टोकन कンテक्स्ट विंडो है। यह मॉडल नेटिव मल्टीमॉडलिटी के साथ आता है, जो एक ही मॉडल में छवियों और वीडियो को संसाधित करता है। हालांकि वजन अभी तक उपलब्ध नहीं हैं, बेंचमार्क परिणाम आशाजनक दिखाते हैं, जो कई अन्य मॉडलों को पीछे छोड़ देते हैं। सस्ते चीनी मॉडलों का युग समाप्त हो सकता है, और 'ओपन' का अर्थ अब कुछ ऐसा है जिसे आप निरीक्षण कर सकते हैं लेकिन शायद कभी चला नहीं सकते।

summary: Khmer Hybrid Calendar: A JavaScript library combining Gregorian and Khmer lunar calendars
content: I'm excited to announce the release of khmer-hybrid-calendar, a library that seamlessly integrates Gregorian and Khmer lunar calendars. title: AI Agents Need SSL: Revolutionizing Trust
title_hi: एआई एजेंटों को अपना एसएसएल चाहिए
summary: AI agents need SSL for secure interactions
content: In 1995, Netscape's SSL transformed the web, enabling secure e-commerce. Today, AI agents face a similar trust crisis. Without a secure way to verify identities, agents can't trust each other. I've built ATC (Agent Trust Card), an SSL equivalent for AI agents, to address this issue. ATC uses Ed25519 signatures and a public key to verify agent identities, ensuring secure interactions between agents.
content_hi: १९९५ में, नेटस्केप के एसएसएल ने वेब को बदल दिया, सुरक्षित ई-कॉमर्स को सक्षम किया। आज, एआई एजेंट एक समान विश्वास संकट का सामना कर रहे हैं। अपनी पहचान को सत्यापित करने के लिए एक सुरक्षित तरीके के बिना, एजेंट एक दूसरे पर विश्वास नहीं कर सकते। मैंने एटीसी (एजेंट ट्रस्ट कार्ड) बनाया है, जो एआई एजेंटों के लिए एसएसएल का एक समकक्ष है, इस मुद्दे को हल करने के लिए।

title: qModel Open-Source Platform v1.2.0 Released
title_hi: qModel ओपन-सोर्स प्लेटफ़ॉर्म v1.2.0 जारी
summary: qModel v1.2.0 simplifies Python model integration
content: The qModel Open-Source Platform v1.2.0 streamlines Python model integration and execution. This release overhauls Python model integration, enabling teams to standardize and manage existing models with confidence. The platform also includes pre-execution validation, checking ZIP directory structure integrity and the presence of main.py.

title: Your Services Already Know Why They Broke
title_hi: आपकी सेवाएँ पहले से ही जानती हैं कि वे क्यों टूट गईं
summary: Services know why they break, but we delete that knowledge at deploy time
content: At 3 a.m., a dashboard is red, and you're trying to figure out which service failed and why. The answer was never lost; it was in the source code. The payment service talks to Postgres through a connection pool, and this retry backs off three times. Autoscaling is good at reacting to resource pressure but has no idea what your app is for. It can't tell a healthy service from one writing garbage to the database.
content_hi: सुबह ३ बजे, एक डैशबोर्ड लाल है, और आप यह जानने की कोशिश कर रहे हैं कि कौन सी सेवा विफल हो गई और क्यों। उत्तर कभी खोया नहीं था; यह स्रोत कोड में था। भुगतान सेवा एक कनेक्शन पूल के माध्यम से पोस्टग्रेस से बात करती है, और यह पुनः प्रयास तीन बार पीछे हट जाता है।

title: San Francisco Mayor Demands Stricter Rules After Waymo Traffic Chaos

summary: San Francisco mayor seeks stricter rules for Waymo
content: San Francisco mayor Daniel Lurie is calling for tougher regulations on robotaxi operators like Waymo following a massive gridlock event. The incident caused hours-long traffic congestion, highlighting the need for more stringent rules. Mayor Lurie has urged state regulators to take action and implement additional requirements to prevent such incidents in the future.
content_hi: सैन फ्रांसिस्को के मेयर डैनियल लुरी ने वेमो जैसे रोबोटैक्सी ऑपरेटरों पर सख्त नियमों की मांग की है। एक बड़े यातायात हंगामे के बाद यह कदम उठाया गया है। मेयर लुरी ने राज्य नियामकों से ऐसी घटनाओं को रोकने के लिए तुरंत कार्रवाई करने का आह्वान किया है।

title: Backrooms Creator Addresses A24 Copyright Strikes

summary: Backrooms director addresses A24 copyright strikes on fan art
content: Kane Parsons, the director of the Backrooms, has responded to concerns over A24 issuing copyright strikes against fan art inspired by the film. The creepypasta-turned-blockbuster has gained a massive following, and fans have been creating artwork based on its unique atmosphere and characters. However, some content creators have received copyright strikes from A24, prompting Parsons to investigate the matter. In a statement, Parsons assured fans that he is 'looking into' the issue and will work to resolve it.
content_hi: बैकरूम्स के निर्देशक केन पार्सन्स ने प्रशंसक कला पर ए24 द्वारा जारी कॉपीराइट स्ट्राइक्स के बारे में चिंताओं का जवाब दिया है। यह फिल्म एक क्रीपीपास्टा से बनी है, जिसने एक बड़ी फॉलोइंग हासिल की है और प्रशंसक इसके अनोखे माहौल और पात्रों पर आधारित कला बना रहे हैं। हालांकि, कुछ सामग्री निर्माताओं को ए24 से कॉपीराइट स्ट्राइक्स मिले हैं, जिसने पार्सन्स को इस मामले की जांच करने पर मजबूर किया है।
